# Blockquotes

## Basic Blockquote

> This is a blockquote.

> This is a blockquote
> that spans multiple lines
> in the source.

## Nested Blockquotes

> First level
>
> > Second level
> >
> > > Third level
> > >
> > > > Fourth level

## Blockquote with Other Elements

> ### Heading in a Blockquote
>
> Paragraph in a blockquote with **bold** and *italic* text.
>
> - List item 1
> - List item 2
>   - Nested item
>
> 1. Ordered item
> 2. Another item

> Code in a blockquote:
>
> ```javascript
> const x = 42;
> console.log(x);
> ```

> | Table | In | Quote |
> |-------|-----|-------|
> | A     | B   | C     |

> [Link in blockquote](https://example.com)
>
> ![Image in blockquote](https://via.placeholder.com/200x50)

## Multi-paragraph Blockquote

> First paragraph of the blockquote. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit.
>
> Second paragraph. Sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua.
>
> Third paragraph. Ut enim ad minim veniam, quis nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris.

## GitHub-style Alerts (if supported)

> [!NOTE]
> This is a note alert.

> [!TIP]
> This is a tip alert.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> This is an important alert.

> [!WARNING]
> This is a warning alert.

> [!CAUTION]
> This is a caution alert.

## Blockquote with Long Content

> This is a very long blockquote line that tests how the renderer handles text wrapping within a blockquote element. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim ad minim veniam, quis nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris nisi ut aliquip ex ea commodo consequat. Duis aute irure dolor in reprehenderit in voluptate velit esse cillum dolore eu fugiat nulla pariatur.
